instruction: Open a text editor and type the sentence: the quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog
Open the Settings app and go to the Display settings page
Open a text editor, type the word hello, and save the file as hello.txt
Open a paint or drawing application and draw a straight line across the canvas
setup: PREINSTALL a paint app on the target (e.g. Drawing or Pinta) and confirm it appears in GNOME search BEFORE this task -- the 2026-07-21 battery voided this task because no paint app existed (operator 2026-07-22: reinstate the Win10 paint task with the app preinstalled). Drag_to exercises the teleport-drag path; if the line doesn't render, that's the known slow-drag gap (appliance.py drag), not the model.

instruction: Read the current time shown in the clock at the top of the screen and tell me what it says
Using the Files application, create a new empty text file in the Home folder and rename it to report.txt, so that a file literally named report.txt exists in the Home folder.
setup: roadmap Phase 2 slice D-b: a longer (~3-subgoal) task with an END-STATE postcondition. No special setup beyond stock GNOME apps.

instruction: Open Settings, switch the appearance theme to Dark, then open the Text Editor so that its window is now rendering with a dark background, confirming the dark theme is applied system-wide.

Read the current time shown in the top bar clock, then open a text editor and save a new file named time.txt whose entire content is that exact time, so the file's content matches what the clock showed.

Open the text editor and type the sentence 'meeting notes: sync at 3pm'. Select and copy that text, open a second text editor document, paste the text into it, and save that second document as notes.txt, so notes.txt's content matches the sentence exactly.
